question_text: Which of the following correctly explains why the Northern Hemisphere experiences summer when it is winter in the Southern Hemisphere?

options.label: A
options.text: The Northern Hemisphere is closer to the Sun during summer

options.label: B
options.text: The tilt of Earth's axis means the Northern Hemisphere is angled towards the Sun in summer, concentrating solar radiation, while the Southern Hemisphere is angled away

options.label: C
options.text: The Sun orbits around the Northern Hemisphere more closely during summer

options.label: D
options.text: The Northern Hemisphere spins faster on its axis, creating more heat

explanation: Earth's 23.5° axial tilt is the key to seasons. When the Northern Hemisphere tilts towards the Sun, it gets more direct sunlight and longer days (summer), while the Southern Hemisphere tilts away (winter). This happens simultaneously on opposite sides of the globe.
